ORDER GRANTING MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 437C

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF SYSCO SAN FRANCISCO, INCORPORATED'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO CCP 437C IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF ESTABLISHES THAT $9,581.60 IS DUE ON THE COMMON COUNT CLAIMS. SEE ABLEMAN DECLARATION. PLAINTIFF SHALL DISMISS THE BREACH OF GUARANTY CAUSE OF ACTION BY THE TIME OF THE HEARING. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DOES NOT ADDRESS THAT CAUSE OF ACTION AND PLAINTIFF DOES NOT SEEK SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IN THE ALTERNATIVE. PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO JUDGMENT ON THE COMMON COUNT CLAIMS. PLAINTIFF DISMISSED THE BREACH OF GUARANTY CAUSE OF ACTION PRIOR TO THE HEARING. NO OPPOSITION WAS FILED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR #5976 =302/MJM

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Pursuant To Ccp 437c / Notice
Off Calendar Sep-02-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RYAN L. DAHM OF THE COOK COLLECTION ATTORNEYS, TEL. NO. 989-4730 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF SYSCO SAN FRANCISCO, INCORPORATED'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Pursuant To Ccp 437c is granted. Plaintiff establishes that $9,581.60 is due on the common count claims. See Ableman declaration. Plaintiff shall dismiss the breach of guaranty cause of action by the time of the hearing. The motion for summary judgment does not address that cause of action and plaintiff does not seek summary adjudication in the alternative. Plaintiff is entitled to judgment on the common count claims. Plaintiff dismissed the breach of guaranty cause of action prior to the hearing. No opposition was filed. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/mjm

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RAYN L. DAHM OF THE COOK COLLECTION ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 989-4730 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
TAMRA WEINKE, DEFENDANT APPEARING IN PRO PER.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF SYSCO SAN FRANCISCO, INC.'s Application For Right To Attach Order and Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment is Denied without prejudice. The moving party has failed to attach a timely proof of service in accordance with Cal. Rule of Court 3.1300(c). Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
